stemHtml: Which statement correctly describes the “iron law” about incorporating human factors into system design?

options.a: Human Factors should be considered only after system design is complete.
options.b: To consider human factors properly at the design stage is costly, but the cost is paid only once; if the operator must compensate for incorrect design in training, the price must be paid every day.
options.c: Human Factors considerations always increase total system cost.
options.d: Training can completely compensate for poor system design.
